content_en:
throng gathered, my uncle asked, 'What are all those people gathered for?’ He was told, '‘Umar has abandoned his religion.’ He headed for the roofless yard and signaled to the people to show he had granted protection to his nephew. People then dispersed. However, I hated to see Muslims being abused, when none of this torture was ever inflicted on me. So I went to my uncle and told him that I would do without his protection. I thus went back in order to be abused and to defend myself, until Islam had the upper hand." (Reported by Al-Bazzaar, AtTabarani, Abu Na*eem and Al-Baihaqi.)

It is reported that [bn ~Abbaas, may Allah be pleased with them both, said: "I asked ~Umar, may Allah be pleased with him, 'Why did the Prophet confer upon you the title 'AlFarooq’ (the distinguisher between right and wrong)'? He answered, 'Hamzah embraced Islam three days before I did. I went to the mosque, where Abu Jahl hurled insults at the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him. Having learned of that, Hamzah grabbed his bow, rushed to the mosque, and made to Quraysh's ring where Abu Jahl was seated. Hamzah leaned against his bow facing Abu Jahl. The latter looked at him and read evil in his eyes. He asked him, ‘What is the matter, Abu “Imarah?' Hamzah cut with his bow one of his two jugular veins causing blood to gush out. However, Quraysh chose not to aggravate the incident lest things should get worse. ~ Umar said: 'The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, was keeping a low profile staying at Al-Arqam's house. Hamzah set out to meet him and embraced Islam right before him. Three days later, I went out and met Al-Makhzoomyy on my way. I asked him, ‘Have you abandoned your ancestors’ religion and followed Muhammad 's creed?' He answered, ‘If I did, so did others you have every right to hold accountable more than me.’ I asked, 'Who?' He returned, 'Your sister and brother-in-law.’ I rushed to their house. The door was closed, and I heard a murmur inside, so I stepped in and asked, ‘What was that I

have just heard?" They answered, ‘You have heard nothing.’ We both continued to exchange words till I struck my brotherin-law's head causing him to bleed. My sister got up, held my head and said, 'You have done this against your will.’ The sight of the blood caused me to grow ashamed of myself. I sat down and said, ‘Show me this leaf.' She replied: it is a book

(V4: Baty) “Loy gaah yy ary" "which none shall touch but those who are clean." (AlWagqi ah: 79) I got up and performed the purificatory bath (Ghusl). They handed me a leaf wherein [ read, 'In the name of Allah, Most Gracious, Most Merciful.' I said, 'These are Sacred and Divine Attributes.' I read, }

(YON aby "asd OT ale ipl w ab”

"Ta Ha. We have not sent down The Quran to thee to be (an occasion) for thy distress" (Ta Ha: 1)

(A cay" gladell tated ai oh Uy ali y ali"

through "To him belong The Most Beautiful names." (Ta Ha: 8) I was awe-stricken. I thought, ‘This is what Quraysh fled from.' Thereupon I embraced Islam, and asked, ‘Where is Allah's Messenger?’ My sister answered, ‘He is at AlArgam's.' I reached Al-Argam's place and knocked on the door. People inside gathered, and Hamzah asked them, 'What is wrong?’ They replied, ‘It is ~Umar.' Hamzah returned, 'Even if it is “Umar, let him in. If he embraces Islam, we will accept him. If he does not, we will kill him.’ Having heard this, the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, came out. At that point, ~Umar pronounced the profession of faith, causing all

people in the house to cry ‘Allah is the Greatest,’ with their words echoing throughout Makkah. I asked, 'O Prophet of Allah, is it not the truth that we believe in? The Prophet answered, 'It is.’ | asked, 'Why is it that we have to hide then?’ We stepped out in two lines, with Hamzah in one and myself in the other until we reached the mosque. Quraysh looked at Hamzah and me and then lapsed in such boundless grief, as they had never experienced before. Since that day the Prophet has called me 'Al-Farooq’. (i.e. the distinguisher between right and wrong.)" This is because “Umar got Islam to see the bright light of day and managed to set right and wrong apart." (Reported by Abu Na‘ eem and Ibn * Aasaakir.)

It is narrated that [bn * Abbaas, may Allah be pleased with him and his father, said: "When *Umar embraced Islam, Gabriel descended on the Prophet and told him, 'O Muhammad, all the dwellers of heaven were delighted at *Umar's embracing Islam.'" (Reported by Ibn Mjah and AlHakim.)

Ibn ~Abbaas, may Allah be pleased with him and his father, is reported to have said: "When * Umar embraced Islam, the disbelievers said, ‘Only today have people (i.e. Muslims) taken vengeance on us.'" (Reported by Al-Bazzaar and AlHakim.) Allah also revealed His words:

"O Prophet! Sufficient unto thee is Allah - (unto thee) and unto those who follow thee among the Believers." (Al-Anfaal: 64)

Ibn Mas*ood, may Allah be pleased with him, is reported to have said: "We have grown powerful since the day

“Umar embraced Islam." On the authority of [bn Sa*d and AtTabarani who reported that Ibn Mas* ood, may Allah be pleased with him, said: "*Umar's conversion to Islam was a conquest, his migration victory and his caliphate mercy. We never dared pray at the Ka* bah before “Umar embraced Islam. After that, he Umar fought them till they were forced to let us pray." (Reported by Al-Bukhari.)

Suhayb is reported to have said: "When *Umar embraced Islam, he brought this religion onto the light and publicly called on people to embrace it. Since then, we have been able to sit in rings around the Ka* bah and circumambulate it. Since then, we have managed to take vengeance on whoever was callous to us and hit back whenever the disbelievers sought to strike us." (Reported by Ibn Sa” d.)

Aslam, ~Umar's bondman, is reported to have said, “( Umar) converted to Islam in Dhul-Hijjah, 6 AH, at the age of twenty six." (Reported by Ibn Sa‘ d.)

‘Umar Migrates Despite the Disbelievers:

“Ali, may Allah be pleased with him, reports: "Never have I known anyone to have emigrated but in secret with the exception of ~ Umar. When he resolved to emigrate, he took his sword, picked up his bow, grabbed some arrows and headed for the Ka* bah where the elite of Quraysh were. He performed the circumambulation, performed two Rak*ahs (i.e prayer units) at Abraham's Station, then stopped at each of their rings and said, 'Misshapened are the faces: let anyone who wants his mother to lose him, his son to be made an orphan and his wife to be widowed, meet me beyond that valley.’ However, nobody dared take one step after him." (Reported by Ibn ~ Aasaakir.)

Al-Buraa', may Allah be pleased with him, is also reported to have said: "The first of the Muhajireen to arrive was Mus*ab Ibn ~Umayr, followed by Ibn Umm Makhtoom, then “Umar Al-Khattaab accompanied by twenty others. We asked, 'What does Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, plan to do?’ He answered, ‘He is soon to follow.’ Then Allah's Messenger and Abu Bakr later arrived."

Hadiths on the Virtues of ~Umar:

Though the man's virtues are simply endless, to the point that whole volumes would not suffice to include them all, it is expedient to bring in the spotlight at least some of them.

The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said: "What an excellent man Abu Bakr is, what an excellent man “Umar is..."

Abu Said Al-Khudryy reports that Allah's Messenger said: "People of higher ranks can be seen by those beneath them the way you can detect a bright star shining in the sky. Abu Bakr and “Umar are among them. How excellent they are!” (Reported by At-Tarmidhi.)

Abu Sa'id Al-Khudryy reports that Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, said: "While I was asleep 1 Saw people being presented to me (in a dream) and they wore shirts and some_ of these reached up to their breasts and some beyond that. Then there happened to pass “Umar Ibn AlKhattaab and_ his shirt had been trailing." They said: "Allah's Messenger, how do you interpret this dream?" He said: "It indicates strength of faith." (Reported by Al-Bukhari and Moslem.)

Abu Hurayrah reports that Allah's Messenger, peace and

blessings be upon him, said: "While J was asleep, I saw myself drawing water from my well in order to quench the thirst of the people; then Abu Bakr came to me. He took hold of the leather bucket from my hand so that he could serve water to the people. He drew two bucketfuls and there was some weakness in his drawing (may Allah forgive him). Then came Ibn Al-Khattaab and he took hold of the bucket, and 1 did not see a person. stronger than him (drawing water) until the people went away with their thirst quenched and the bucket filled with water." (Reported by Al-Bukhari and Moslem.)

Abu Hurayrah reports that Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, said: "There had been among the people before you Muhaddathun (i.e. inspired persons) and if there were any such among my nation “Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab would be one of them." (Reported by Al-Bukhari and Moslem.) AtTarmidhi said that according to Ibn ~ Uyaynah, Muhaddathun means Muffahhamun (i.e. those endowed with Divine understanding), while in his version, Moslem adds: "Ibn Wahb explains that the word Muhaddathun refers to those who receive inspiration from heaven (Mulhamun)." This is the majority of opinions according to Ibn Hajar who points: "Muhaddathun means spoken to by the angels. According to Ibn Al-Atheer: Muhaddathun means people who are right if they rely on their guessing and intuition, so it is as if they had been informed of the right opinion. The Hadith also included an explanation of the word as Mulhamun (i.e. inspired). An inspired person is one who is informed of something and so speaks of it relying on his intuition and insight. Allah bestows this on only some of His servants such as “Umar, may Allah be pleased with him. The bottom-line is that * Umar was granted the gift of true inspiration which is the characteristic of Allah's Friends; it is called Kashf (unveiling).

Ibn *Umar narrates: Allah's Messenger, peace and

blessings be upon him, said: "Allah has set the truth on “Umar's tongue and in his heart." (Reported by At-Tarmidhi.) Ibn ~Umar also mentions: "Whenever people encountered a certain situation concerning which their opinions varied, the Quran was revealed to support ~Umar's." (Reported by AtTarmidhi.)
content_id:
kerumunan berkumpul, paman saya bertanya, 'Mengapa semua orang itu berkumpul?' Dia diberitahu, ''Umar telah meninggalkan agamanya.' Dia menuju ke halaman tanpa atap dan memberi isyarat kepada orang-orang untuk menunjukkan bahwa dia telah memberikan perlindungan kepada keponakannya. Orang-orang pun bubar. Namun, aku membenci melihat kaum Muslimin disakiti, sementara siksaan itu tidak pernah menimpaku. Maka aku menemui pamanku dan memberitahunya bahwa aku tidak memerlukan perlindungannya. Aku pun kembali untuk menerima siksaan dan membela diri, hingga akhirnya Islam memperoleh kemenangan." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bazzaar, At-Thabarani, Abu Nu'aim, dan Al-Baihaqi.)

Diriwayatkan bahwa Ibnu 'Abbas, semoga Allah meridhai keduanya, berkata: "Aku bertanya kepada 'Umar, semoga Allah meridhainya, 'Mengapa Nabi memberimu gelar 'Al-Faruq' (pembeda antara yang haq dan yang bathil)'? Dia menjawab, 'Hamzah masuk Islam tiga hari sebelum aku. Aku pergi ke masjid, di mana Abu Jahl mencaci maki Nabi, shalawat dan salam atasnya. Setelah mendengar hal itu, Hamzah mengambil busurnya, bergegas ke masjid, dan mendatangi lingkaran Quraisy tempat Abu Jahl duduk. Hamzah bersandar pada busurnya menghadap Abu Jahl. Abu Jahl menatapnya dan membaca keburukan di matanya. Dia bertanya kepadanya, 'Ada apa, Abu 'Imarah?' Hamzah memotong dengan busurnya salah satu dari dua urat leher Abu Jahl hingga darah mengalir deras. Namun, Quraisy memilih untuk tidak memperkeruh keadaan agar tidak semakin buruk. 'Umar berkata: 'Nabi, shalawat dan salam atasnya, bersembunyi di rumah Al-Arqam. Hamzah pergi menemuinya dan masuk Islam langsung di hadapannya. Tiga hari kemudian, aku keluar dan bertemu Al-Makhzumiy di jalan. Aku bertanya kepadanya, 'Apakah engkau telah meninggalkan agama nenek moyangmu dan mengikuti ajaran Muhammad?' Dia menjawab, 'Jika aku melakukannya, demikian pula orang lain yang lebih berhak kaupertanyakan daripadaku.' Aku bertanya, 'Siapa?' Dia menjawab, 'Saudara perempuanmu dan suaminya.' Aku bergegas ke rumah mereka. Pintu tertutup, dan aku mendengar suara samar di dalam, maka aku masuk dan bertanya, 'Suara apa yang baru

saja kudengar?' Mereka menjawab, 'Engkau tidak mendengar apa-apa.' Kami berdua terus berdebat hingga aku memukul kepala iparku hingga berdarah. Saudara perempuanku bangkit, memegang kepalaku dan berkata, 'Engkau melakukan ini tanpa kehendakmu sendiri.' Melihat darah itu membuatku malu pada diri sendiri. Aku duduk dan berkata, 'Tunjukkan lembaran itu kepadaku.' Dia menjawab: ini adalah kitab

(V4: Baty) "Loy gaah yy ary" "yang tidak boleh disentuh kecuali oleh mereka yang suci." (Al-Waqi'ah: 79) Aku bangkit dan melakukan mandi (Ghusl). Mereka menyerahkan selembar kertas kepadaku dan di dalamnya aku membaca, 'Dengan nama Allah, Maha Pengasih, Maha Penyayang.' Aku berkata, 'Ini adalah Sifat-sifat yang Suci dan Ilahi.' Aku membaca, }

(YON aby "asd OT ale ipl w ab"

"Thaa Haa. Kami tidak menurunkan Al-Quran ini kepadamu agar engkau menjadi susah." (Thaha: 1)

(A cay" gladell tated ai oh Uy ali y ali"

hingga "Bagi-Nya nama-nama yang paling indah." (Thaha: 8) Aku diliputi rasa kagum. Aku berpikir, 'Inilah yang membuat Quraisy melarikan diri.' Seketika itu aku masuk Islam, dan bertanya, 'Di mana Rasulullah?' Saudara perempuanku menjawab, 'Beliau ada di Al-Arqam.' Aku sampai di tempat Al-Arqam dan mengetuk pintu. Orang-orang di dalam berkumpul, dan Hamzah bertanya kepada mereka, 'Ada apa?' Mereka menjawab, 'Itu 'Umar.' Hamzah menjawab, 'Meskipun itu 'Umar, biarkan dia masuk. Jika dia masuk Islam, kita terima dia. Jika tidak, kita bunuh dia.' Setelah mendengar ini, Nabi, shalawat dan salam atasnya, keluar. Pada saat itu, 'Umar mengucapkan syahadat, membuat semua

orang di rumah itu berseru 'Allahu Akbar,' dengan suara mereka bergema di seluruh Makkah. Aku bertanya, 'Wahai Nabi Allah, bukankah kita berada di atas kebenaran? Nabi menjawab, 'Ya.' Aku bertanya, 'Mengapa kita harus bersembunyi?' Kami keluar dalam dua barisan, dengan Hamzah di salah satunya dan aku di yang lain hingga kami sampai di masjid. Quraisy memandang Hamzah dan aku lalu tenggelam dalam kesedihan yang begitu dalam, sebagaimana belum pernah mereka rasakan sebelumnya. Sejak hari itulah Nabi memanggilku 'Al-Faruq'. (yakni pembeda antara yang haq dan yang bathil.)" Ini karena 'Umar membawa Islam ke cahaya yang terang dan berhasil memisahkan yang benar dari yang salah." (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Nu'aim dan Ibnu 'Asakir.)

Diriwayatkan bahwa Ibnu 'Abbas, semoga Allah meridhai dia dan ayahnya, berkata: "Ketika 'Umar masuk Islam, Jibril turun kepada Nabi dan berkata kepadanya, 'Wahai Muhammad, semua penghuni langit bergembira atas masuk Islamnya 'Umar.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Majah dan Al-Hakim.)

Ibnu 'Abbas, semoga Allah meridhai dia dan ayahnya, dilaporkan telah berkata: "Ketika 'Umar masuk Islam, orang-orang kafir berkata, 'Baru hari inilah orang-orang (yakni kaum Muslimin) membalas dendam kepada kami.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bazzaar dan Al-Hakim.) Allah juga menurunkan firman-Nya:

"Wahai Nabi! Cukuplah Allah bagimu — bagimu dan bagi orang-orang yang mengikutimu di antara kaum mukminin." (Al-Anfal: 64)

Ibnu Mas'ud, semoga Allah meridhainya, dilaporkan telah berkata: "Kami semakin kuat sejak hari

'Umar masuk Islam." Berdasarkan riwayat Ibnu Sa'd dan At-Thabarani yang melaporkan bahwa Ibnu Mas'ud, semoga Allah meridhainya, berkata: "Masuk Islamnya 'Umar adalah sebuah penaklukan, hijrahnya adalah kemenangan, dan kekhalifahannya adalah rahmat. Kami tidak pernah berani shalat di Ka'bah sebelum 'Umar masuk Islam. Setelah itu, 'Umar memerangi mereka hingga mereka terpaksa membiarkan kami shalat." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari.)

Shuhaib dilaporkan telah berkata: "Ketika 'Umar masuk Islam, dia membawa agama ini ke cahaya dan secara terang-terangan mengajak orang-orang untuk memeluknya. Sejak saat itu, kami dapat duduk dalam lingkaran di sekitar Ka'bah dan melakukan thawaf. Sejak saat itu, kami berhasil membalas siapa pun yang berlaku keras kepada kami dan membalas setiap kali orang-orang kafir berusaha menyerang kami." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Sa'd.)

Aslam, budak 'Umar, dilaporkan telah berkata, "'Umar masuk Islam pada bulan Dzulhijjah, tahun 6 H, pada usia dua puluh enam tahun." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Sa'd.)

'Umar Berhijrah Meskipun Dihalangi Orang-orang Kafir:

'Ali, semoga Allah meridhainya, melaporkan: "Belum pernah aku mengetahui seseorang yang berhijrah kecuali secara sembunyi-sembunyi, kecuali 'Umar. Ketika dia bertekad untuk berhijrah, dia mengambil pedangnya, mengambil busurnya, meraih beberapa anak panah dan menuju Ka'bah tempat para pembesar Quraisy berada. Dia melakukan thawaf, melakukan dua raka'at (yakni unit shalat) di Maqam Ibrahim, kemudian berhenti di setiap lingkaran mereka dan berkata, 'Buruklah wajah-wajah itu: siapa pun yang ingin ibunya kehilangan dirinya, anaknya menjadi yatim, dan istrinya menjadi janda, temuilah aku di balik lembah itu.' Namun, tidak ada seorang pun yang berani mengambil satu langkah pun di belakangnya." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu 'Asakir.)

Al-Barra', semoga Allah meridhainya, juga dilaporkan telah berkata: "Yang pertama dari kalangan Muhajirin yang tiba adalah Mush'ab Ibnu 'Umayr, diikuti oleh Ibnu Umm Maktum, kemudian 'Umar Al-Khattab ditemani dua puluh orang lainnya. Kami bertanya, 'Apa rencana Rasulullah, shalawat dan salam atasnya?' Dia menjawab, 'Beliau akan segera menyusul.' Kemudian Rasulullah dan Abu Bakr pun tiba."

Hadith-hadith tentang Keutamaan 'Umar:

Meskipun keutamaan beliau sungguh tak terbatas, hingga beberapa jilid pun tidak akan cukup untuk memuat semuanya, namun sudah selayaknya untuk menyoroti setidaknya sebagian darinya.

Nabi, shalawat dan salam atasnya, bersabda: "Alangkah mulianya Abu Bakr, alangkah mulianya 'Umar..."

Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah bersabda: "Orang-orang yang memiliki kedudukan lebih tinggi dapat dilihat oleh mereka yang berada di bawahnya sebagaimana kalian dapat melihat bintang terang yang bersinar di langit. Abu Bakr dan 'Umar termasuk di antara mereka. Alangkah mulianya mereka!" (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidzi.)

Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah, shalawat dan salam atasnya, bersabda: "Ketika aku sedang tidur, aku melihat orang-orang diperlihatkan kepadaku (dalam mimpi) dan mereka mengenakan baju gamis; sebagian baju gamis itu mencapai dadanya dan sebagian lagi melebihi itu. Kemudian lewatlah 'Umar Ibnu Al-Khattab dan baju gamisnya menjuntai." Mereka bertanya: "Wahai Rasulullah, bagaimana engkau menafsirkan mimpi ini?" Beliau bersabda: "Itu menunjukkan kekuatan iman." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dan Muslim.)

Abu Hurairah melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah, shalawat dan

salam atasnya, bersabda: "Ketika aku sedang tidur, aku melihat diriku menimba air dari sumurku untuk memuaskan dahaga orang-orang; kemudian Abu Bakr datang kepadaku. Dia mengambil timba kulit dari tanganku agar dapat memberikan air kepada orang-orang. Dia menimba dua kali timba dan ada sedikit kelemahan dalam timbanya (semoga Allah mengampuninya). Kemudian datanglah Ibnu Al-Khattab dan dia mengambil timba itu, dan aku tidak melihat seseorang yang lebih kuat darinya (dalam menimba air) hingga orang-orang pergi dengan dahaga mereka terpuaskan dan timba penuh dengan air." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dan Muslim.)

Abu Hurayrah meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda: "Dahulu di antara umat-umat sebelum kalian terdapat Muhaddathun (yaitu orang-orang yang mendapat ilham), dan jika ada seseorang seperti itu di antara umatku, maka 'Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab adalah salah seorang dari mereka." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dan Muslim.) At-Tirmidzi berkata bahwa menurut Ibn 'Uyaynah, Muhaddathun berarti Muffahhamun (yaitu mereka yang dianugerahi pemahaman ilahi), sementara dalam riwayatnya, Muslim menambahkan: "Ibn Wahb menjelaskan bahwa kata Muhaddathun merujuk kepada mereka yang menerima ilham dari langit (Mulhamun)." Ini adalah pendapat mayoritas menurut Ibn Hajar yang menunjukkan: "Muhaddathun berarti orang-orang yang diajak bicara oleh para malaikat. Menurut Ibn Al-Atheer: Muhaddathun berarti orang-orang yang benar jika mereka bersandar pada dugaan dan intuisi mereka, sehingga seolah-olah mereka telah diberi tahu pendapat yang benar. Hadith tersebut juga mencakup penjelasan kata tersebut sebagai Mulhamun (yaitu yang mendapat ilham). Orang yang mendapat ilham adalah seseorang yang diberi tahu tentang sesuatu sehingga ia berbicara tentangnya berdasarkan intuisi dan penilaiannya. Allah menganugerahkan ini hanya kepada sebagian hamba-Nya seperti 'Umar, radhiyallahu 'anhu. Kesimpulannya adalah bahwa 'Umar dianugerahi karunia ilham yang benar yang merupakan ciri khas para wali Allah; hal ini disebut Kashf (penyingkapan).

Ibn 'Umar meriwayatkan: Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa

sallam, bersabda: "Allah telah menjadikan kebenaran berada pada lisan dan hati 'Umar." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidzi.) Ibn 'Umar juga menyebutkan: "Setiap kali orang-orang menghadapi suatu situasi yang pendapat mereka berbeda-beda mengenainya, Al-Quran diturunkan untuk mendukung pendapat 'Umar." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidzi.)
content_ar:
يتناول هذا الفصل من "سيرة الخلفاء الراشدين" جانبًا من جوانب حياة أحد الخلفاء الراشدين الكرام، مُبيّنًا مواقفه في خلافته وما اتسم به من عدل وتقوى وحرص على مصلحة المسلمين.

وتستعرض هذه السيرة دروسًا قيادية وتربوية عظيمة من حياة هذا الخليفة الراشد الذي سار على منهج النبي ﷺ في الحكم والقضاء وإقامة العدل ونشر الإسلام في ربوع الأرض.
